data.title: As a developer, escalation to a higher model tier is permanent within a review cycle
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the one-way ratchet for model tier escalation and replace the existing deep-tier re-review escalation (pass 3+ → full deep pipeline) with the new ESCALATE_REVIEW mechanism.
**Why**: The deep tier evaluates different criteria than the original reviewer, creating apples-to-oranges comparisons on escalation. The ratchet ensures consistency.
**Scope**:
- IN: Ratchet state tracking in review workflow, re-review model selection respects ratchet, removal of pass 3+ → full deep pipeline logic from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, pass 2 behavior preserved for non-escalated reviews
- OUT: ESCALATE_REVIEW signal definition (Story 3). approach_viability_concern routing (Story 6).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, once a review escalates to a higher model tier via ESCALATE_REVIEW, all subsequent re-reviews for that change use the escalated tier or above
  ← Satisfies: "Escalation is a one-way ratchet: once a review escalates to a higher model tier, all subsequent re-reviews for that change remain at the escalated tier or above"
- When this story is complete, the pass 3+ → full deep pipeline escalation logic is removed from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and replaced by the ESCALATE_REVIEW ratchet mechanism
  ← Satisfies: "The current deep-tier re-review escalation (pass 3+ → full deep pipeline) is replaced by the within-review ESCALATE_REVIEW mechanism"
- When this story is complete, pass 2 re-review behavior for non-escalated reviews is preserved (light→standard upgrade, deep→full deep pipeline)
  ← Satisfies: "The current deep-tier re-review escalation is replaced" (preservation of non-affected behavior)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md escalation table (lines 546-571) controls both pass 2 and pass 3+ behavior — the boundary of what to remove vs. preserve must be explicit: remove pass 3+ auto-escalation, preserve pass 2 tier-specific behavior
- [Adversarial] Story 6 also modifies the Autonomous Resolution Loop — coordinate insertion points to avoid shared-state conflicts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
